title: Homepage Open Graph image uses SVG, risking blank social previews
reasoning: The homepage explicitly opts into `/og/home.svg` as its social preview asset. Many Open Graph and social preview consumers do not reliably render SVG image assets, so the page-specific preview can fail or fall back even though the production polish spec requires a composed homepage preview image.
reproduction: Run the homepage URL through a social preview validator or share target that rejects SVG `og:image` assets; the preview may render blank or fall back instead of showing the composed card.
recommendation: Generate a raster `public/og/home.png` or JPG from the same composition and point the homepage `ogImage` at that asset; keep the SVG only as an editable source if desired.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No metadata/share-card tests or preview validation artifacts are included.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a metadata/static assertion that high-value page `ogImage` values resolve to crawler-safe raster formats such as PNG or JPG.
minimumFixScope: Add a raster homepage OG asset and change `src/pages/index.astro` to reference it.

history.note: Implemented in working tree; native checks pass. Full Clawpatch provider revalidation was curtailed after confirming first five fixes because it was too slow.
